prompt: Architectural bird's-eye view of a perfectly symmetrical circular amphitheater. At the exact center stands a single small human figure, alone. The amphitheater has six radial corridors extending outward. In each corridor, a dense crowd of people moves away from the center, toward the periphery. Each crowd member's posture is directed outward. At the far end of each corridor, an even larger crowd is visible in the open space beyond. The composition creates a sense of centrifugal social gravity — the center is the least inhabited point in a system where everyone belongs to a larger cluster than the one they appear to anchor. Photorealistic aerial perspective, soft overcast light, architectural precision. No text, no labels, no symbols.
visual_logic: A lone figure at a plaza's center sees crowds through every arch; each crowd faces outward toward larger gatherings — peripherality is recursive, universal, inescapable.
